Title: ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
ï»¿
ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
Welcome to ICCBDC 2026 @ University of Cambridge, Cambridge, U.K.
Big data is a rapidly expanding research area spanning the fields of computer science and information management, and has become a ubiquitous term in understanding and solving complex problems in different disciplinary fields such as engineering, applied mathematics, medicine, computational biology, healthcare, social networks, finance, business, government, education, transportation and telecommunications.
We sincerely invite you to participate
2026 the 10th  International Conference on Cloud and Big Data Computing (ICCBDC 2026)
. It'll be held at
University of Cambridge, Cambridge, United Kingdom during August 13-15, 2026
.
Since 2017, ICCBDC has been an annual event which was held in many places, University of Northumbria London Campus, Barcelona, St Anne's College in University of Oxford, Birmingham City University, Manchester Metropolitan University, Oxford Brookes University and University of Manchester. In 2026, ICCBDC will be held in Cambridge. The University of Cambridge is rich in history - its famous Colleges and University buildings attract visitors from all over the world.
Conference Proceedings
ICCBDC 2026 welcomes author submission of papers concerning any branch of the Cloud and Big Data, Communication Systems, and other subjects. The subjects include Big Data Algorithms, Applications and Services, Cloud and Big Data for Internet of Things (IoT),
More Topics
All academic papers will be submitted to the technical committee for double-blind peer review according to the reviewing policies. After proper registration and presentation, will be published in
ICCBDC Conference Proceedings
,
which will be archived in the
ACM Digital Library
, and be indexed by
Ei Compendex and Scopus
and submitted to be reviewed by Thomson Reuters Conference Proceedings Citation Index (ISI Web of Science).

Link: Venue Location
Title: ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
ï»¿
ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
Venue
Location
Conference Venue
University of Cambridge
The University of Cambridge is rich in history - its famous Colleges and University buildings attract visitors from all over the world. But the University's museums and collections also hold many treasures which give an exciting insight into some of the scholarly activities, both past and present, of the University's academics and students.
The University of Cambridge is one of the oldest universities in the world and one of the largest in the United Kingdom. Its reputation for outstanding academic achievement is known world-wide and reflects the intellectual achievement of its students, as well as the world-class original research carried out by the staff of the University and the Colleges. Its reputation is endorsed by the Quality Assurance Agency and by other external reviewers of learning and teaching, such as External Examiners. These high standards are the result of both the learning opportunities offered at Cambridge and by its extensive resources, including libraries, museums and other collections. Teaching consists not only of lectures, seminars and practical classes led by people who are world experts in their field, but also more personalised teaching arranged through the Colleges. Many opportunities exist for students to interact with scholars of all levels, both formally and informally.

Title: ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
ï»¿
ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
Visa
Visa
Visa Information
Not required for Australian, Canadian, New Zealand and US visitors, as well as several other nations, for stays of up to six months.
More Information
Immigration to the UK is becoming tougher, particularly for those seeking to work or study. The exit of the UK from the EU will take several years, but the process has commenced and entry requirements for EU nationals may change. Make sure you check the website of the UK Border Agency (www.gov.uk/check-uk-visa) or with your local British embassy or consulate for the most up-to-date information.

Link: Submission Guideline
Title: ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
ï»¿
ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
Submission
Submission
Submission Guide to Authors
Submission Deadline of Full Paper:
May 20, 2026
Submission Deadline of Abstract:
May 20, 2026
Notification of Review Result: June 20, 2026
Registration Deadline:
July 10, 2026

Main Conference Date: August 13-15, 2026
If you wish to submit a paper to ICCBDC 2026, please use the following Online submission System.
Â» Click Here to Log in Online System to submit your paper (.pdf)
Paper Submission:
Authors are invited to submit an unpublished original work that must not have been submitted to an other conference, workshop or journal. All papers will be peer-reviewed by members of the Program Committee and be evaluated for originality, significance of the contribution, technical correctness and presentation.
Formatting and Publication Instructions:
Conference papers need to be prepared according to conference proceedings format.
Link to Manuscript Templates for Conference Proceedings
Review Criteria:
The degree to which the paper deals with the above issues is fundamental to a successful review and selection of the paper. The most frequent cause of rejections of submitted papers is a lack of new results. Duplicated submissions to concurrent conferences must be avoided. All submissions will be reviewed by the Program Committee on the basis of technical quality, relevance to scope of the conference, originality, significance, and clarity.
Relevance
:	Is the topic of the paper relevant to the scope of ICCBDC? Does it show the potential to stimulate interactive discussion?
Originality
:	How novel and innovative is the paper?
Significance
:	Does the paper make a valuable contribution to the theory or the practice of Cloud and Big Data Computing?
Clarity
: Is the paper well presented and organised?
Accepted papers will be published in the conference proceedings. All manuscripts are submitted as full papers and are reviewed based on their scientific merit. The reviewing process is confidential. All paper submissions will be carefully reviewed by TPC experts and reviews will be returned to the author(s) with comments to ensure the high quality of the accepted papers. The accepted papers must be revised, taking into consideration the referees' comments and suggestions, before inclusion in the conference proceedings.

Important Registration Notes:
Â» Student Registration: A student claiming a student registration must submit:

or
a verification letter of the studentâs full-time status from the department head of the studentâs current institution or the studentâs supervisor

Â» All registrations include: conference kits (name badge, bag, certificate, conference program, souvenir), admission to all technical programs (keynote talks, parallel sessions for paper presentations, etc.), 2 times coffee breaks (August 15), and lunch and dinner for the August 14.

Refunds Policy
If a registrant is unable to attend an event for any reason, they may substitute, by arrangement with the registrar, someone else of Co-authors or from the same institute/organization.
Attendants who cancel their registration until May 15, 2026 will obtain a 70% refund of the paid fee.
After May 15, 2026 no cancellations are possible. Registration fees covering the publication of one or more papers CANNOT be cancelled.
Personal Reason
The ICCBDC cannot refund or pay any compensation where could not attend the conference is prevented by reason of circumstances which amount to âpersonal reasonâ. Such as travel difficulties, visa problems, health issues, financial default etc.
Force Majeure
The ICCBDC cannot accept responsibility, refund or pay any compensation where the performance of the conference is prevented or affected by reason of circumstances which amount to âforce majeureâ.
Circumstances amounting to âforce majeureâ include any event which we could not, even with all due care, foresee or avoid. Such circumstances include the fire, flood, explosion, storm or other weather damage, break-in, criminal damage, riots or civil strife, industrial action, natural or nuclear disaster, fire, adverse weather conditions, war or threat of war, actual or threatened terrorist activity, epidemic and all similar situations beyond our control.
Attention!
For safety consideration, please take good care of your belongings in the public places. Please bring your name badge for entering the conference hall. Please do not lend your name badge to people who are not involved with the conference. Please do not take irrelevant people to enter the conference rooms. The Organizers shall not be held responsibility for any loss of personal finance or belongings.
Â» Please note ICCBDC 2026 Conferences and the organizing committee of the conference are not authorized to assist with the VISA process beyond providing the Notification of Invitation Letter issued by ICCBDC 2026 Conferences and conference Committee Board. Should your application be denied, ICCBDC 2026 Conferences and the organizing committee of the conference cannot change the decision of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, nor will ICCBDC 2026 Conferences and the organizing committee of the conference engage in discussion or correspondence with the MOFA or the Embassy on behalf of the applicant. The registration fee cannot be refunded when the VISA application of individual being denied.

=== PAGE: https://www.iccbdc.org/welcome.html ===
Link: Welcome from Chair
Title: ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
ï»¿
ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
Welcome
Letter from Conference Chair
Welcome Letter from Conference Chair
We sincerely invite you to participate 2026 the 10th International Conference on Cloud and Big Data Computing.  It'll be held at University of Cambridge, Cambridge, United Kingdom during August 13-15, 2026.
The goal is to establish an international forum for engineers and scientists to present their ideas and experiences in the fields of Cloud and Big Data Computing like we always do.
The ICCBDC 2026 topics include but are not limited to the following. Authors are encouraged to submit unpublished original research works in one of the following topics.
Scope and Topics
Foundations of Cloud and Big Data Systems
Cloud-Native and Distributed System Architectures
Virtualization, Containerization, and Resource Orchestration
Big Data Processing Frameworks and Query Engines
Distributed Storage and Data Management Technologies
Performance, Scalability, and Resource Management
Intelligence and Analytics on Cloud and Big Data
Advanced Analytics, AI/ML Platforms, and Data Mining
Operational Excellence: Monitoring, Automation (AIOps/MLOps), and FinOps
Security, Privacy, and Compliance in Cloud and Big Data Environments
Data Governance, Quality, and Observability
AI-Enabled Cloud and Big Data Analytics and Services
Generative AI and Large Language Models on Cloud Platforms
Scalable AI/ML Model Training and Serving Infrastructure
AI Agents and Autonomous Systems for Data Workflows
AI-Native Application Development and Architecture
Multimodal Data Analysis with Foundation Models
MLOps, LLMOps, and Model Governance at Scale
Applications and Evolution of Cloud and Big Data Computing
Cloud and Big Data for IoT, Edge Computing, and Emerging Domains
Industry Practices, Business Transformation, and Case Studies
Sustainable and Green Cloud and Big Data Computing
Future Trends: Ethical AI, Quantum Computing, and Societal Impact

Title: ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
ï»¿
ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
Welcome to ICCBDC 2026 @ University of Cambridge, Cambridge, U.K.
Big data is a rapidly expanding research area spanning the fields of computer science and information management, and has become a ubiquitous term in understanding and solving complex problems in different disciplinary fields such as engineering, applied mathematics, medicine, computational biology, healthcare, social networks, finance, business, government, education, transportation and telecommunications.
We sincerely invite you to participate
2026 the 10th  International Conference on Cloud and Big Data Computing (ICCBDC 2026)
. It'll be held at
University of Cambridge, Cambridge, United Kingdom during August 13-15, 2026
.
Since 2017, ICCBDC has been an annual event which was held in many places, University of Northumbria London Campus, Barcelona, St Anne's College in University of Oxford, Birmingham City University, Manchester Metropolitan University, Oxford Brookes University and University of Manchester. In 2026, ICCBDC will be held in Cambridge. The University of Cambridge is rich in history - its famous Colleges and University buildings attract visitors from all over the world.
Conference Proceedings
ICCBDC 2026 welcomes author submission of papers concerning any branch of the Cloud and Big Data, Communication Systems, and other subjects. The subjects include Big Data Algorithms, Applications and Services, Cloud and Big Data for Internet of Things (IoT),
More Topics
All academic papers will be submitted to the technical committee for double-blind peer review according to the reviewing policies. After proper registration and presentation, will be published in
ICCBDC Conference Proceedings
,
which will be archived in the
ACM Digital Library
, and be indexed by
Ei Compendex and Scopus
and submitted to be reviewed by Thomson Reuters Conference Proceedings Citation Index (ISI Web of Science).

=== PAGE: https://www.iccbdc.org/welcome.html ===
Link: Welcome from Chair
Title: ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
ï»¿
ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
Welcome
Letter from Conference Chair
Welcome Letter from Conference Chair
We sincerely invite you to participate 2026 the 10th International Conference on Cloud and Big Data Computing.  It'll be held at University of Cambridge, Cambridge, United Kingdom during August 13-15, 2026.
The goal is to establish an international forum for engineers and scientists to present their ideas and experiences in the fields of Cloud and Big Data Computing like we always do.
The ICCBDC 2026 topics include but are not limited to the following. Authors are encouraged to submit unpublished original research works in one of the following topics.
Scope and Topics
Foundations of Cloud and Big Data Systems
Cloud-Native and Distributed System Architectures
Virtualization, Containerization, and Resource Orchestration
Big Data Processing Frameworks and Query Engines
Distributed Storage and Data Management Technologies
Performance, Scalability, and Resource Management
Intelligence and Analytics on Cloud and Big Data
Advanced Analytics, AI/ML Platforms, and Data Mining
Operational Excellence: Monitoring, Automation (AIOps/MLOps), and FinOps
Security, Privacy, and Compliance in Cloud and Big Data Environments
Data Governance, Quality, and Observability
AI-Enabled Cloud and Big Data Analytics and Services
Generative AI and Large Language Models on Cloud Platforms
Scalable AI/ML Model Training and Serving Infrastructure
AI Agents and Autonomous Systems for Data Workflows
AI-Native Application Development and Architecture
Multimodal Data Analysis with Foundation Models
MLOps, LLMOps, and Model Governance at Scale
Applications and Evolution of Cloud and Big Data Computing
Cloud and Big Data for IoT, Edge Computing, and Emerging Domains
Industry Practices, Business Transformation, and Case Studies
Sustainable and Green Cloud and Big Data Computing
Future Trends: Ethical AI, Quantum Computing, and Societal Impact

ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
Call for Papers
Call for Papers
Call for Papers
The ICCBDC 2026 topics include but are not limited to the following. Authors are encouraged to submit unpublished original research works. Topics of interest for submission include, but are not limited to:
Foundations of Cloud and Big Data Systems
Cloud-Native and Distributed System Architectures
Virtualization, Containerization, and Resource Orchestration
Big Data Processing Frameworks and Query Engines
Distributed Storage and Data Management Technologies
Performance, Scalability, and Resource Management
Intelligence and Analytics on Cloud and Big Data
Advanced Analytics, AI/ML Platforms, and Data Mining
Operational Excellence: Monitoring, Automation (AIOps/MLOps), and FinOps
Security, Privacy, and Compliance in Cloud and Big Data Environments
Data Governance, Quality, and Observability
AI-Enabled Cloud and Big Data Analytics and Services
Generative AI and Large Language Models on Cloud Platforms
Scalable AI/ML Model Training and Serving Infrastructure
AI Agents and Autonomous Systems for Data Workflows
AI-Native Application Development and Architecture
Multimodal Data Analysis with Foundation Models
MLOps, LLMOps, and Model Governance at Scale
Applications and Evolution of Cloud and Big Data Computing
Cloud and Big Data for IoT, Edge Computing, and Emerging Domains
Industry Practices, Business Transformation, and Case Studies
Sustainable and Green Cloud and Big Data Computing
Future Trends: Ethical AI, Quantum Computing, and Societal Impact

If you wish to submit a paper to ICCBDC 2026, please use the following Online submission System.
Â» Click Here to Log in Online System to submit your paper (.pdf)
Paper Submission:
Authors are invited to submit an unpublished original work that must not have been submitted to an other conference, workshop or journal. All papers will be peer-reviewed by members of the Program Committee and be evaluated for originality, significance of the contribution, technical correctness and presentation.
Formatting and Publication Instructions:
Conference papers need to be prepared according to conference proceedings format.
Link to Manuscript Templates for Conference Proceedings
Review Criteria:
The degree to which the paper deals with the above issues is fundamental to a successful review and selection of the paper. The most frequent cause of rejections of submitted papers is a lack of new results. Duplicated submissions to concurrent conferences must be avoided. All submissions will be reviewed by the Program Committee on the basis of technical quality, relevance to scope of the conference, originality, significance, and clarity.
Relevance
:	Is the topic of the paper relevant to the scope of ICCBDC? Does it show the potential to stimulate interactive discussion?
Originality
:	How novel and innovative is the paper?
Significance
:	Does the paper make a valuable contribution to the theory or the practice of Cloud and Big Data Computing?
Clarity
: Is the paper well presented and organised?
Accepted papers will be published in the conference proceedings. All manuscripts are submitted as full papers and are reviewed based on their scientific merit. The reviewing process is confidential. All paper submissions will be carefully reviewed by TPC experts and reviews will be returned to the author(s) with comments to ensure the high quality of the accepted papers. The accepted papers must be revised, taking into consideration the referees' comments and suggestions, before inclusion in the conference proceedings.

Refunds Policy
If a registrant is unable to attend an event for any reason, they may substitute, by arrangement with the registrar, someone else of Co-authors or from the same institute/organization.
Attendants who cancel their registration until May 15, 2026 will obtain a 70% refund of the paid fee.
After May 15, 2026 no cancellations are possible. Registration fees covering the publication of one or more papers CANNOT be cancelled.
Personal Reason
The ICCBDC cannot refund or pay any compensation where could not attend the conference is prevented by reason of circumstances which amount to âpersonal reasonâ. Such as travel difficulties, visa problems, health issues, financial default etc.
Force Majeure
The ICCBDC cannot accept responsibility, refund or pay any compensation where the performance of the conference is prevented or affected by reason of circumstances which amount to âforce majeureâ.
Circumstances amounting to âforce majeureâ include any event which we could not, even with all due care, foresee or avoid. Such circumstances include the fire, flood, explosion, storm or other weather damage, break-in, criminal damage, riots or civil strife, industrial action, natural or nuclear disaster, fire, adverse weather conditions, war or threat of war, actual or threatened terrorist activity, epidemic and all similar situations beyond our control.
Attention!
For safety consideration, please take good care of your belongings in the public places. Please bring your name badge for entering the conference hall. Please do not lend your name badge to people who are not involved with the conference. Please do not take irrelevant people to enter the conference rooms. The Organizers shall not be held responsibility for any loss of personal finance or belongings.

Link: Venue Location
Title: ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
ï»¿
ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
Venue
Location
Conference Venue
University of Cambridge
The University of Cambridge is rich in history - its famous Colleges and University buildings attract visitors from all over the world. But the University's museums and collections also hold many treasures which give an exciting insight into some of the scholarly activities, both past and present, of the University's academics and students.
The University of Cambridge is one of the oldest universities in the world and one of the largest in the United Kingdom. Its reputation for outstanding academic achievement is known world-wide and reflects the intellectual achievement of its students, as well as the world-class original research carried out by the staff of the University and the Colleges. Its reputation is endorsed by the Quality Assurance Agency and by other external reviewers of learning and teaching, such as External Examiners. These high standards are the result of both the learning opportunities offered at Cambridge and by its extensive resources, including libraries, museums and other collections. Teaching consists not only of lectures, seminars and practical classes led by people who are world experts in their field, but also more personalised teaching arranged through the Colleges. Many opportunities exist for students to interact with scholars of all levels, both formally and informally.

Call for Papers
Call for Papers
The ICCBDC 2026 topics include but are not limited to the following. Authors are encouraged to submit unpublished original research works. Topics of interest for submission include, but are not limited to:
Foundations of Cloud and Big Data Systems
Cloud-Native and Distributed System Architectures
Virtualization, Containerization, and Resource Orchestration
Big Data Processing Frameworks and Query Engines
Distributed Storage and Data Management Technologies
Performance, Scalability, and Resource Management
Intelligence and Analytics on Cloud and Big Data
Advanced Analytics, AI/ML Platforms, and Data Mining
Operational Excellence: Monitoring, Automation (AIOps/MLOps), and FinOps
Security, Privacy, and Compliance in Cloud and Big Data Environments
Data Governance, Quality, and Observability
AI-Enabled Cloud and Big Data Analytics and Services
Generative AI and Large Language Models on Cloud Platforms
Scalable AI/ML Model Training and Serving Infrastructure
AI Agents and Autonomous Systems for Data Workflows
AI-Native Application Development and Architecture
Multimodal Data Analysis with Foundation Models
MLOps, LLMOps, and Model Governance at Scale
Applications and Evolution of Cloud and Big Data Computing
Cloud and Big Data for IoT, Edge Computing, and Emerging Domains
Industry Practices, Business Transformation, and Case Studies
Sustainable and Green Cloud and Big Data Computing
Future Trends: Ethical AI, Quantum Computing, and Societal Impact

Link: Submission Guideline
Title: ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
ï»¿
ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
Submission
Submission
Submission Guide to Authors
Submission Deadline of Full Paper:
May 20, 2026
Submission Deadline of Abstract:
May 20, 2026
Notification of Review Result: June 20, 2026
Registration Deadline:
July 10, 2026
Final Paper Due Date: July 15, 2026
Main Conference Date: August 13-15, 2026
If you wish to submit a paper to ICCBDC 2026, please use the following Online submission System.
Â» Click Here to Log in Online System to submit your paper (.pdf)
Paper Submission:
Authors are invited to submit an unpublished original work that must not have been submitted to an other conference, workshop or journal. All papers will be peer-reviewed by members of the Program Committee and be evaluated for originality, significance of the contribution, technical correctness and presentation.
Formatting and Publication Instructions:
Conference papers need to be prepared according to conference proceedings format.
Link to Manuscript Templates for Conference Proceedings
Review Criteria:
The degree to which the paper deals with the above issues is fundamental to a successful review and selection of the paper. The most frequent cause of rejections of submitted papers is a lack of new results. Duplicated submissions to concurrent conferences must be avoided. All submissions will be reviewed by the Program Committee on the basis of technical quality, relevance to scope of the conference, originality, significance, and clarity.
Relevance
:	Is the topic of the paper relevant to the scope of ICCBDC? Does it show the potential to stimulate interactive discussion?
Originality
:	How novel and innovative is the paper?
Significance
:	Does the paper make a valuable contribution to the theory or the practice of Cloud and Big Data Computing?
Clarity
: Is the paper well presented and organised?
Accepted papers will be published in the conference proceedings. All manuscripts are submitted as full papers and are reviewed based on their scientific merit. The reviewing process is confidential. All paper submissions will be carefully reviewed by TPC experts and reviews will be returned to the author(s) with comments to ensure the high quality of the accepted papers. The accepted papers must be revised, taking into consideration the referees' comments and suggestions, before inclusion in the conference proceedings.

Welcome
Letter from Conference Chair
Welcome Letter from Conference Chair
We sincerely invite you to participate 2026 the 10th International Conference on Cloud and Big Data Computing.  It'll be held at University of Cambridge, Cambridge, United Kingdom during August 13-15, 2026.
The goal is to establish an international forum for engineers and scientists to present their ideas and experiences in the fields of Cloud and Big Data Computing like we always do.
The ICCBDC 2026 topics include but are not limited to the following. Authors are encouraged to submit unpublished original research works in one of the following topics.

Foundations of Cloud and Big Data Systems
Cloud-Native and Distributed System Architectures
Virtualization, Containerization, and Resource Orchestration
Big Data Processing Frameworks and Query Engines
Distributed Storage and Data Management Technologies
Performance, Scalability, and Resource Management
Intelligence and Analytics on Cloud and Big Data
Advanced Analytics, AI/ML Platforms, and Data Mining
Operational Excellence: Monitoring, Automation (AIOps/MLOps), and FinOps
Security, Privacy, and Compliance in Cloud and Big Data Environments
Data Governance, Quality, and Observability
AI-Enabled Cloud and Big Data Analytics and Services
Generative AI and Large Language Models on Cloud Platforms
Scalable AI/ML Model Training and Serving Infrastructure
AI Agents and Autonomous Systems for Data Workflows
AI-Native Application Development and Architecture
Multimodal Data Analysis with Foundation Models
MLOps, LLMOps, and Model Governance at Scale
Applications and Evolution of Cloud and Big Data Computing
Cloud and Big Data for IoT, Edge Computing, and Emerging Domains
Industry Practices, Business Transformation, and Case Studies
Sustainable and Green Cloud and Big Data Computing
Future Trends: Ethical AI, Quantum Computing, and Societal Impact

Refunds Policy
If a registrant is unable to attend an event for any reason, they may substitute, by arrangement with the registrar, someone else of Co-authors or from the same institute/organization.
Attendants who cancel their registration until May 15, 2026 will obtain a 70% refund of the paid fee.
After May 15, 2026 no cancellations are possible. Registration fees covering the publication of one or more papers CANNOT be cancelled.
Personal Reason
The ICCBDC cannot refund or pay any compensation where could not attend the conference is prevented by reason of circumstances which amount to âpersonal reasonâ. Such as travel difficulties, visa problems, health issues, financial default etc.
Force Majeure
The ICCBDC cannot accept responsibility, refund or pay any compensation where the performance of the conference is prevented or affected by reason of circumstances which amount to âforce majeureâ.
Circumstances amounting to âforce majeureâ include any event which we could not, even with all due care, foresee or avoid. Such circumstances include the fire, flood, explosion, storm or other weather damage, break-in, criminal damage, riots or civil strife, industrial action, natural or nuclear disaster, fire, adverse weather conditions, war or threat of war, actual or threatened terrorist activity, epidemic and all similar situations beyond our control.
Attention!
For safety consideration, please take good care of your belongings in the public places. Please bring your name badge for entering the conference hall. Please do not lend your name badge to people who are not involved with the conference. Please do not take irrelevant people to enter the conference rooms. The Organizers shall not be held responsibility for any loss of personal finance or belongings.

Should your application be denied, ICCBDC 2026 Conferences and the organizing committee of the conference cannot change the decision of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, nor will ICCBDC 2026 Conferences and the organizing committee of the conference engage in discussion or correspondence with the MOFA or the Embassy on behalf of the applicant. Link: Venue Location
Title: ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
ï»¿
ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
Venue
Location
Conference Venue
University of Cambridge
The University of Cambridge is rich in history - its famous Colleges and University buildings attract visitors from all over the world. But the University's museums and collections also hold many treasures which give an exciting insight into some of the scholarly activities, both past and present, of the University's academics and students.
The University of Cambridge is one of the oldest universities in the world and one of the largest in the United Kingdom. Its reputation for outstanding academic achievement is known world-wide and reflects the intellectual achievement of its students, as well as the world-class original research carried out by the staff of the University and the Colleges. Its reputation is endorsed by the Quality Assurance Agency and by other external reviewers of learning and teaching, such as External Examiners. These high standards are the result of both the learning opportunities offered at Cambridge and by its extensive resources, including libraries, museums and other collections. Teaching consists not only of lectures, seminars and practical classes led by people who are world experts in their field, but also more personalised teaching arranged through the Colleges. Many opportunities exist for students to interact with scholars of all levels, both formally and informally.

ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
Welcome to ICCBDC 2026 @ University of Cambridge, Cambridge, U.K.
Big data is a rapidly expanding research area spanning the fields of computer science and information management, and has become a ubiquitous term in understanding and solving complex problems in different disciplinary fields such as engineering, applied mathematics, medicine, computational biology, healthcare, social networks, finance, business, government, education, transportation and telecommunications.
We sincerely invite you to participate
2026 the 10th  International Conference on Cloud and Big Data Computing (ICCBDC 2026)
. It'll be held at
University of Cambridge, Cambridge, United Kingdom during August 13-15, 2026
.
Since 2017, ICCBDC has been an annual event which was held in many places, University of Northumbria London Campus, Barcelona, St Anne's College in University of Oxford, Birmingham City University, Manchester Metropolitan University, Oxford Brookes University and University of Manchester. In 2026, ICCBDC will be held in Cambridge. The University of Cambridge is rich in history - its famous Colleges and University buildings attract visitors from all over the world.
Conference Proceedings
ICCBDC 2026 welcomes author submission of papers concerning any branch of the Cloud and Big Data, Communication Systems, and other subjects. The subjects include Big Data Algorithms, Applications and Services, Cloud and Big Data for Internet of Things (IoT),
More Topics
All academic papers will be submitted to the technical committee for double-blind peer review according to the reviewing policies. After proper registration and presentation, will be published in
ICCBDC Conference Proceedings
,

ACM Digital Library
, and be indexed by
Ei Compendex and Scopus
and submitted to be reviewed by Thomson Reuters Conference Proceedings Citation Index (ISI Web of Science).

=== PAGE: https://www.iccbdc.org/welcome.html ===
Link: Welcome from Chair
Title: ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
ï»¿
ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
Welcome
Letter from Conference Chair
Welcome Letter from Conference Chair
We sincerely invite you to participate 2026 the 10th International Conference on Cloud and Big Data Computing.  It'll be held at University of Cambridge, Cambridge, United Kingdom during August 13-15, 2026.
The goal is to establish an international forum for engineers and scientists to present their ideas and experiences in the fields of Cloud and Big Data Computing like we always do.
The ICCBDC 2026 topics include but are not limited to the following. Authors are encouraged to submit unpublished original research works in one of the following topics.
Scope and Topics
Foundations of Cloud and Big Data Systems
Cloud-Native and Distributed System Architectures
Virtualization, Containerization, and Resource Orchestration
Big Data Processing Frameworks and Query Engines
Distributed Storage and Data Management Technologies
Performance, Scalability, and Resource Management
Intelligence and Analytics on Cloud and Big Data
Advanced Analytics, AI/ML Platforms, and Data Mining
Operational Excellence: Monitoring, Automation (AIOps/MLOps), and FinOps
Security, Privacy, and Compliance in Cloud and Big Data Environments
Data Governance, Quality, and Observability
AI-Enabled Cloud and Big Data Analytics and Services
Generative AI and Large Language Models on Cloud Platforms
Scalable AI/ML Model Training and Serving Infrastructure
AI Agents and Autonomous Systems for Data Workflows
AI-Native Application Development and Architecture
Multimodal Data Analysis with Foundation Models
MLOps, LLMOps, and Model Governance at Scale
Applications and Evolution of Cloud and Big Data Computing
Cloud and Big Data for IoT, Edge Computing, and Emerging Domains
Industry Practices, Business Transformation, and Case Studies
Sustainable and Green Cloud and Big Data Computing
Future Trends: Ethical AI, Quantum Computing, and Societal Impact

Link: Paper Topics
Title: ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
ï»¿
ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
Call for Papers
Call for Papers
Call for Papers
The ICCBDC 2026 topics include but are not limited to the following. Authors are encouraged to submit unpublished original research works. Topics of interest for submission include, but are not limited to:
Foundations of Cloud and Big Data Systems
Cloud-Native and Distributed System Architectures
Virtualization, Containerization, and Resource Orchestration
Big Data Processing Frameworks and Query Engines
Distributed Storage and Data Management Technologies
Performance, Scalability, and Resource Management
Intelligence and Analytics on Cloud and Big Data
Advanced Analytics, AI/ML Platforms, and Data Mining
Operational Excellence: Monitoring, Automation (AIOps/MLOps), and FinOps
Security, Privacy, and Compliance in Cloud and Big Data Environments
Data Governance, Quality, and Observability
AI-Enabled Cloud and Big Data Analytics and Services
Generative AI and Large Language Models on Cloud Platforms
Scalable AI/ML Model Training and Serving Infrastructure
AI Agents and Autonomous Systems for Data Workflows
AI-Native Application Development and Architecture
Multimodal Data Analysis with Foundation Models
MLOps, LLMOps, and Model Governance at Scale
Applications and Evolution of Cloud and Big Data Computing
Cloud and Big Data for IoT, Edge Computing, and Emerging Domains
Industry Practices, Business Transformation, and Case Studies
Sustainable and Green Cloud and Big Data Computing
Future Trends: Ethical AI, Quantum Computing, and Societal Impact

If you wish to submit a paper to ICCBDC 2026, please use the following Online submission System.
Â» Click Here to Log in Online System to submit your paper (.pdf)
Paper Submission:
Authors are invited to submit an unpublished original work that must not have been submitted to an other conference, workshop or journal. All papers will be peer-reviewed by members of the Program Committee and be evaluated for originality, significance of the contribution, technical correctness and presentation.
Formatting and Publication Instructions:
Conference papers need to be prepared according to conference proceedings format.
Link to Manuscript Templates for Conference Proceedings
Review Criteria:
The degree to which the paper deals with the above issues is fundamental to a successful review and selection of the paper. The most frequent cause of rejections of submitted papers is a lack of new results. Duplicated submissions to concurrent conferences must be avoided. All submissions will be reviewed by the Program Committee on the basis of technical quality, relevance to scope of the conference, originality, significance, and clarity.
Relevance
:	Is the topic of the paper relevant to the scope of ICCBDC? Does it show the potential to stimulate interactive discussion?
Originality
:	How novel and innovative is the paper?
Significance
:	Does the paper make a valuable contribution to the theory or the practice of Cloud and Big Data Computing?
Clarity
: Is the paper well presented and organised?
Accepted papers will be published in the conference proceedings. All manuscripts are submitted as full papers and are reviewed based on their scientific merit. The reviewing process is confidential. All paper submissions will be carefully reviewed by TPC experts and reviews will be returned to the author(s) with comments to ensure the high quality of the accepted papers. The accepted papers must be revised, taking into consideration the referees' comments and suggestions, before inclusion in the conference proceedings.

Refunds Policy
If a registrant is unable to attend an event for any reason, they may substitute, by arrangement with the registrar, someone else of Co-authors or from the same institute/organization.
Attendants who cancel their registration until May 15, 2026 will obtain a 70% refund of the paid fee.
After May 15, 2026 no cancellations are possible. Registration fees covering the publication of one or more papers CANNOT be cancelled.
Personal Reason
The ICCBDC cannot refund or pay any compensation where could not attend the conference is prevented by reason of circumstances which amount to âpersonal reasonâ. Such as travel difficulties, visa problems, health issues, financial default etc.
Force Majeure
The ICCBDC cannot accept responsibility, refund or pay any compensation where the performance of the conference is prevented or affected by reason of circumstances which amount to âforce majeureâ.
Circumstances amounting to âforce majeureâ include any event which we could not, even with all due care, foresee or avoid. Such circumstances include the fire, flood, explosion, storm or other weather damage, break-in, criminal damage, riots or civil strife, industrial action, natural or nuclear disaster, fire, adverse weather conditions, war or threat of war, actual or threatened terrorist activity, epidemic and all similar situations beyond our control.
Attention!
For safety consideration, please take good care of your belongings in the public places. Please bring your name badge for entering the conference hall. Please do not lend your name badge to people who are not involved with the conference. Please do not take irrelevant people to enter the conference rooms. The Organizers shall not be held responsibility for any loss of personal finance or belongings.

Link: Venue Location
Title: ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK

ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
Venue
Location
Conference Venue
University of Cambridge
The University of Cambridge is rich in history - its famous Colleges and University buildings attract visitors from all over the world. But the University's museums and collections also hold many treasures which give an exciting insight into some of the scholarly activities, both past and present, of the University's academics and students.
The University of Cambridge is one of the oldest universities in the world and one of the largest in the United Kingdom. Its reputation for outstanding academic achievement is known world-wide and reflects the intellectual achievement of its students, as well as the world-class original research carried out by the staff of the University and the Colleges. Its reputation is endorsed by the Quality Assurance Agency and by other external reviewers of learning and teaching, such as External Examiners. These high standards are the result of both the learning opportunities offered at Cambridge and by its extensive resources, including libraries, museums and other collections. Teaching consists not only of lectures, seminars and practical classes led by people who are world experts in their field, but also more personalised teaching arranged through the Colleges. Many opportunities exist for students to interact with scholars of all levels, both formally and informally.

Big data is a rapidly expanding research area spanning the fields of computer science and information management, and has become a ubiquitous term in understanding and solving complex problems in different disciplinary fields such as engineering, applied mathematics, medicine, computational biology, healthcare, social networks, finance, business, government, education, transportation and telecommunications.

Link: Submission Guideline
Title: ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
ï»¿
ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
Submission
Submission
Submission Guide to Authors
Submission Deadline of Full Paper:
May 20, 2026
Submission Deadline of Abstract:
May 20, 2026
Notification of Review Result: June 20, 2026
Registration Deadline:
July 10, 2026
Final Paper Due Date: July 15, 2026
Main Conference Date: August 13-15, 2026
If you wish to submit a paper to ICCBDC 2026, please use the following Online submission System.
Â» Click Here to Log in Online System to submit your paper (.pdf)
Paper Submission:
Authors are invited to submit an unpublished original work that must not have been submitted to an other conference, workshop or journal. All papers will be peer-reviewed by members of the Program Committee and be evaluated for originality, significance of the contribution, technical correctness and presentation.
Formatting and Publication Instructions:
Conference papers need to be prepared according to conference proceedings format.
Link to Manuscript Templates for Conference Proceedings
Review Criteria:
The degree to which the paper deals with the above issues is fundamental to a successful review and selection of the paper. The most frequent cause of rejections of submitted papers is a lack of new results. Duplicated submissions to concurrent conferences must be avoided. All submissions will be reviewed by the Program Committee on the basis of technical quality, relevance to scope of the conference, originality, significance, and clarity.
Relevance
:	Is the topic of the paper relevant to the scope of ICCBDC? Does it show the potential to stimulate interactive discussion?
Originality
:	How novel and innovative is the paper?
Significance
:	Does the paper make a valuable contribution to the theory or the practice of Cloud and Big Data Computing?
Clarity
: Is the paper well presented and organised?
Accepted papers will be published in the conference proceedings. All manuscripts are submitted as full papers and are reviewed based on their scientific merit. The reviewing process is confidential. All paper submissions will be carefully reviewed by TPC experts and reviews will be returned to the author(s) with comments to ensure the high quality of the accepted papers. The accepted papers must be revised, taking into consideration the referees' comments and suggestions, before inclusion in the conference proceedings.

or

Refunds Policy
If a registrant is unable to attend an event for any reason, they may substitute, by arrangement with the registrar, someone else of Co-authors or from the same institute/organization.
Attendants who cancel their registration until May 15, 2026 will obtain a 70% refund of the paid fee.
After May 15, 2026 no cancellations are possible. Registration fees covering the publication of one or more papers CANNOT be cancelled.
Personal Reason
The ICCBDC cannot refund or pay any compensation where could not attend the conference is prevented by reason of circumstances which amount to âpersonal reasonâ. Such as travel difficulties, visa problems, health issues, financial default etc.
Force Majeure
The ICCBDC cannot accept responsibility, refund or pay any compensation where the performance of the conference is prevented or affected by reason of circumstances which amount to âforce majeureâ.
Circumstances amounting to âforce majeureâ include any event which we could not, even with all due care, foresee or avoid. Such circumstances include the fire, flood, explosion, storm or other weather damage, break-in, criminal damage, riots or civil strife, industrial action, natural or nuclear disaster, fire, adverse weather conditions, war or threat of war, actual or threatened terrorist activity, epidemic and all similar situations beyond our control.
Attention!
For safety consideration, please take good care of your belongings in the public places. Please bring your name badge for entering the conference hall. Please do not lend your name badge to people who are not involved with the conference. Please do not take irrelevant people to enter the conference rooms. The Organizers shall not be held responsibility for any loss of personal finance or belongings.
Â» Please note ICCBDC 2026 Conferences and the organizing committee of the conference are not authorized to assist with the VISA process beyond providing the Notification of Invitation Letter issued by ICCBDC 2026 Conferences and conference Committee Board. Should your application be denied, ICCBDC 2026 Conferences and the organizing committee of the conference cannot change the decision of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, nor will ICCBDC 2026 Conferences and the organizing committee of the conference engage in discussion or correspondence with the MOFA or the Embassy on behalf of the applicant. The registration fee cannot be refunded when the VISA application of individual being denied.

Link: Welcome from Chair
Title: ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
ï»¿
ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
Welcome
Letter from Conference Chair
Welcome Letter from Conference Chair
We sincerely invite you to participate 2026 the 10th International Conference on Cloud and Big Data Computing.  It'll be held at University of Cambridge, Cambridge, United Kingdom during August 13-15, 2026.
The goal is to establish an international forum for engineers and scientists to present their ideas and experiences in the fields of Cloud and Big Data Computing like we always do.
The ICCBDC 2026 topics include but are not limited to the following. Authors are encouraged to submit unpublished original research works in one of the following topics.
Scope and Topics
Foundations of Cloud and Big Data Systems
Cloud-Native and Distributed System Architectures
Virtualization, Containerization, and Resource Orchestration
Big Data Processing Frameworks and Query Engines
Distributed Storage and Data Management Technologies
Performance, Scalability, and Resource Management
Intelligence and Analytics on Cloud and Big Data
Advanced Analytics, AI/ML Platforms, and Data Mining
Operational Excellence: Monitoring, Automation (AIOps/MLOps), and FinOps
Security, Privacy, and Compliance in Cloud and Big Data Environments
Data Governance, Quality, and Observability
AI-Enabled Cloud and Big Data Analytics and Services
Generative AI and Large Language Models on Cloud Platforms
Scalable AI/ML Model Training and Serving Infrastructure
AI Agents and Autonomous Systems for Data Workflows
AI-Native Application Development and Architecture
Multimodal Data Analysis with Foundation Models
MLOps, LLMOps, and Model Governance at Scale
Applications and Evolution of Cloud and Big Data Computing
Cloud and Big Data for IoT, Edge Computing, and Emerging Domains
Industry Practices, Business Transformation, and Case Studies
Sustainable and Green Cloud and Big Data Computing
Future Trends: Ethical AI, Quantum Computing, and Societal Impact

Link: Venue Location
Title: ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
ï»¿
ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
Venue
Location
Conference Venue
University of Cambridge
The University of Cambridge is rich in history - its famous Colleges and University buildings attract visitors from all over the world. But the University's museums and collections also hold many treasures which give an exciting insight into some of the scholarly activities, both past and present, of the University's academics and students.
The University of Cambridge is one of the oldest universities in the world and one of the largest in the United Kingdom. Its reputation for outstanding academic achievement is known world-wide and reflects the intellectual achievement of its students, as well as the world-class original research carried out by the staff of the University and the Colleges. Its reputation is endorsed by the Quality Assurance Agency and by other external reviewers of learning and teaching, such as External Examiners. These high standards are the result of both the learning opportunities offered at Cambridge and by its extensive resources, including libraries, museums and other collections. Teaching consists not only of lectures, seminars and practical classes led by people who are world experts in their field, but also more personalised teaching arranged through the Colleges. Many opportunities exist for students to interact with scholars of all levels, both formally and informally.

Visa
Visa
Visa Information
Not required for Australian, Canadian, New Zealand and US visitors, as well as several other nations, for stays of up to six months.
More Information
Immigration to the UK is becoming tougher, particularly for those seeking to work or study. The exit of the UK from the EU will take several years, but the process has commenced and entry requirements for EU nationals may change. Make sure you check the website of the UK Border Agency (www.gov.uk/check-uk-visa) or with your local British embassy or consulate for the most up-to-date information.

Welcome to ICCBDC 2026 @ University of Cambridge, Cambridge, U.K.
Big data is a rapidly expanding research area spanning the fields of computer science and information management, and has become a ubiquitous term in understanding and solving complex problems in different disciplinary fields such as engineering, applied mathematics, medicine, computational biology, healthcare, social networks, finance, business, government, education, transportation and telecommunications.
We sincerely invite you to participate
2026 the 10th  International Conference on Cloud and Big Data Computing (ICCBDC 2026)
. It'll be held at
University of Cambridge, Cambridge, United Kingdom during August 13-15, 2026
.
Since 2017, ICCBDC has been an annual event which was held in many places, University of Northumbria London Campus, Barcelona, St Anne's College in University of Oxford, Birmingham City University, Manchester Metropolitan University, Oxford Brookes University and University of Manchester. In 2026, ICCBDC will be held in Cambridge. The University of Cambridge is rich in history - its famous Colleges and University buildings attract visitors from all over the world.
Conference Proceedings
ICCBDC 2026 welcomes author submission of papers concerning any branch of the Cloud and Big Data, Communication Systems, and other subjects. The subjects include Big Data Algorithms, Applications and Services, Cloud and Big Data for Internet of Things (IoT),

ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
Welcome
Letter from Conference Chair
Welcome Letter from Conference Chair
We sincerely invite you to participate 2026 the 10th International Conference on Cloud and Big Data Computing.  It'll be held at University of Cambridge, Cambridge, United Kingdom during August 13-15, 2026.
The goal is to establish an international forum for engineers and scientists to present their ideas and experiences in the fields of Cloud and Big Data Computing like we always do.
The ICCBDC 2026 topics include but are not limited to the following. Authors are encouraged to submit unpublished original research works in one of the following topics.
Scope and Topics
Foundations of Cloud and Big Data Systems
Cloud-Native and Distributed System Architectures
Virtualization, Containerization, and Resource Orchestration
Big Data Processing Frameworks and Query Engines
Distributed Storage and Data Management Technologies
Performance, Scalability, and Resource Management
Intelligence and Analytics on Cloud and Big Data
Advanced Analytics, AI/ML Platforms, and Data Mining
Operational Excellence: Monitoring, Automation (AIOps/MLOps), and FinOps
Security, Privacy, and Compliance in Cloud and Big Data Environments
Data Governance, Quality, and Observability
AI-Enabled Cloud and Big Data Analytics and Services
Generative AI and Large Language Models on Cloud Platforms
Scalable AI/ML Model Training and Serving Infrastructure
AI Agents and Autonomous Systems for Data Workflows
AI-Native Application Development and Architecture
Multimodal Data Analysis with Foundation Models
MLOps, LLMOps, and Model Governance at Scale
Applications and Evolution of Cloud and Big Data Computing
Cloud and Big Data for IoT, Edge Computing, and Emerging Domains
Industry Practices, Business Transformation, and Case Studies
Sustainable and Green Cloud and Big Data Computing
Future Trends: Ethical AI, Quantum Computing, and Societal Impact

Submission Guide to Authors
Submission Deadline of Full Paper:

Submission Deadline of Abstract:

If you wish to submit a paper to ICCBDC 2026, please use the following Online submission System.
Â» Click Here to Log in Online System to submit your paper (.pdf)
Paper Submission:
Authors are invited to submit an unpublished original work that must not have been submitted to an other conference, workshop or journal. All papers will be peer-reviewed by members of the Program Committee and be evaluated for originality, significance of the contribution, technical correctness and presentation.
Formatting and Publication Instructions:
Conference papers need to be prepared according to conference proceedings format.
Link to Manuscript Templates for Conference Proceedings
Review Criteria:
The degree to which the paper deals with the above issues is fundamental to a successful review and selection of the paper. The most frequent cause of rejections of submitted papers is a lack of new results. Duplicated submissions to concurrent conferences must be avoided. All submissions will be reviewed by the Program Committee on the basis of technical quality, relevance to scope of the conference, originality, significance, and clarity.
Relevance
:	Is the topic of the paper relevant to the scope of ICCBDC? Does it show the potential to stimulate interactive discussion?
Originality
:	How novel and innovative is the paper?
Significance
:	Does the paper make a valuable contribution to the theory or the practice of Cloud and Big Data Computing?
Clarity
: Is the paper well presented and organised?
Accepted papers will be published in the conference proceedings. All manuscripts are submitted as full papers and are reviewed based on their scientific merit. The reviewing process is confidential. All paper submissions will be carefully reviewed by TPC experts and reviews will be returned to the author(s) with comments to ensure the high quality of the accepted papers. The accepted papers must be revised, taking into consideration the referees' comments and suggestions, before inclusion in the conference proceedings.

Refunds Policy
If a registrant is unable to attend an event for any reason, they may substitute, by arrangement with the registrar, someone else of Co-authors or from the same institute/organization.
Attendants who cancel their registration until May 15, 2026 will obtain a 70% refund of the paid fee.
After May 15, 2026 no cancellations are possible. Registration fees covering the publication of one or more papers CANNOT be cancelled.
Personal Reason
The ICCBDC cannot refund or pay any compensation where could not attend the conference is prevented by reason of circumstances which amount to âpersonal reasonâ. Such as travel difficulties, visa problems, health issues, financial default etc.
Force Majeure
The ICCBDC cannot accept responsibility, refund or pay any compensation where the performance of the conference is prevented or affected by reason of circumstances which amount to âforce majeureâ.
Circumstances amounting to âforce majeureâ include any event which we could not, even with all due care, foresee or avoid. Such circumstances include the fire, flood, explosion, storm or other weather damage, break-in, criminal damage, riots or civil strife, industrial action, natural or nuclear disaster, fire, adverse weather conditions, war or threat of war, actual or threatened terrorist activity, epidemic and all similar situations beyond our control.
Attention!
For safety consideration, please take good care of your belongings in the public places. Please bring your name badge for entering the conference hall. Please do not lend your name badge to people who are not involved with the conference. Please do not take irrelevant people to enter the conference rooms. The Organizers shall not be held responsibility for any loss of personal finance or belongings.

Link: Venue Location
Title: ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
ï»¿
ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
Venue
Location
Conference Venue
University of Cambridge
The University of Cambridge is rich in history - its famous Colleges and University buildings attract visitors from all over the world. But the University's museums and collections also hold many treasures which give an exciting insight into some of the scholarly activities, both past and present, of the University's academics and students.
The University of Cambridge is one of the oldest universities in the world and one of the largest in the United Kingdom. Its reputation for outstanding academic achievement is known world-wide and reflects the intellectual achievement of its students, as well as the world-class original research carried out by the staff of the University and the Colleges. Its reputation is endorsed by the Quality Assurance Agency and by other external reviewers of learning and teaching, such as External Examiners. These high standards are the result of both the learning opportunities offered at Cambridge and by its extensive resources, including libraries, museums and other collections. Teaching consists not only of lectures, seminars and practical classes led by people who are world experts in their field, but also more personalised teaching arranged through the Colleges. Many opportunities exist for students to interact with scholars of all levels, both formally and informally.

Submission
Submission Guide to Authors

May 20, 2026

If you wish to submit a paper to ICCBDC 2026, please use the following Online submission System.
Â» Click Here to Log in Online System to submit your paper (.pdf)
Paper Submission:
Authors are invited to submit an unpublished original work that must not have been submitted to an other conference, workshop or journal. All papers will be peer-reviewed by members of the Program Committee and be evaluated for originality, significance of the contribution, technical correctness and presentation.
Formatting and Publication Instructions:
Conference papers need to be prepared according to conference proceedings format.
Link to Manuscript Templates for Conference Proceedings
Review Criteria:
The degree to which the paper deals with the above issues is fundamental to a successful review and selection of the paper. The most frequent cause of rejections of submitted papers is a lack of new results. Duplicated submissions to concurrent conferences must be avoided. All submissions will be reviewed by the Program Committee on the basis of technical quality, relevance to scope of the conference, originality, significance, and clarity.
Relevance
:	Is the topic of the paper relevant to the scope of ICCBDC? Does it show the potential to stimulate interactive discussion?
Originality
:	How novel and innovative is the paper?
Significance
:	Does the paper make a valuable contribution to the theory or the practice of Cloud and Big Data Computing?
Clarity
: Is the paper well presented and organised?
Accepted papers will be published in the conference proceedings. All manuscripts are submitted as full papers and are reviewed based on their scientific merit. The reviewing process is confidential. All paper submissions will be carefully reviewed by TPC experts and reviews will be returned to the author(s) with comments to ensure the high quality of the accepted papers. The accepted papers must be revised, taking into consideration the referees' comments and suggestions, before inclusion in the conference proceedings.

Refunds Policy
If a registrant is unable to attend an event for any reason, they may substitute, by arrangement with the registrar, someone else of Co-authors or from the same institute/organization.
Attendants who cancel their registration until May 15, 2026 will obtain a 70% refund of the paid fee.
After May 15, 2026 no cancellations are possible. Registration fees covering the publication of one or more papers CANNOT be cancelled.
Personal Reason
The ICCBDC cannot refund or pay any compensation where could not attend the conference is prevented by reason of circumstances which amount to âpersonal reasonâ. Such as travel difficulties, visa problems, health issues, financial default etc.
Force Majeure
The ICCBDC cannot accept responsibility, refund or pay any compensation where the performance of the conference is prevented or affected by reason of circumstances which amount to âforce majeureâ.
Circumstances amounting to âforce majeureâ include any event which we could not, even with all due care, foresee or avoid. Such circumstances include the fire, flood, explosion, storm or other weather damage, break-in, criminal damage, riots or civil strife, industrial action, natural or nuclear disaster, fire, adverse weather conditions, war or threat of war, actual or threatened terrorist activity, epidemic and all similar situations beyond our control.
Attention!
For safety consideration, please take good care of your belongings in the public places. Please bring your name badge for entering the conference hall. Please do not lend your name badge to people who are not involved with the conference. Please do not take irrelevant people to enter the conference rooms. The Organizers shall not be held responsibility for any loss of personal finance or belongings.

Title: ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK

ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
Venue
Location
Conference Venue
University of Cambridge
The University of Cambridge is rich in history - its famous Colleges and University buildings attract visitors from all over the world. But the University's museums and collections also hold many treasures which give an exciting insight into some of the scholarly activities, both past and present, of the University's academics and students.
The University of Cambridge is one of the oldest universities in the world and one of the largest in the United Kingdom. Its reputation for outstanding academic achievement is known world-wide and reflects the intellectual achievement of its students, as well as the world-class original research carried out by the staff of the University and the Colleges. Its reputation is endorsed by the Quality Assurance Agency and by other external reviewers of learning and teaching, such as External Examiners. These high standards are the result of both the learning opportunities offered at Cambridge and by its extensive resources, including libraries, museums and other collections. Teaching consists not only of lectures, seminars and practical classes led by people who are world experts in their field, but also more personalised teaching arranged through the Colleges. Many opportunities exist for students to interact with scholars of all levels, both formally and informally.

Link: Welcome from Chair
Title: ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
ï»¿
ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
Welcome
Letter from Conference Chair
Welcome Letter from Conference Chair
We sincerely invite you to participate 2026 the 10th International Conference on Cloud and Big Data Computing.  It'll be held at University of Cambridge, Cambridge, United Kingdom during August 13-15, 2026.
The goal is to establish an international forum for engineers and scientists to present their ideas and experiences in the fields of Cloud and Big Data Computing like we always do.
The ICCBDC 2026 topics include but are not limited to the following. Authors are encouraged to submit unpublished original research works in one of the following topics.
Scope and Topics
Foundations of Cloud and Big Data Systems
Cloud-Native and Distributed System Architectures
Virtualization, Containerization, and Resource Orchestration
Big Data Processing Frameworks and Query Engines
Distributed Storage and Data Management Technologies
Performance, Scalability, and Resource Management
Intelligence and Analytics on Cloud and Big Data
Advanced Analytics, AI/ML Platforms, and Data Mining
Operational Excellence: Monitoring, Automation (AIOps/MLOps), and FinOps
Security, Privacy, and Compliance in Cloud and Big Data Environments
Data Governance, Quality, and Observability
AI-Enabled Cloud and Big Data Analytics and Services
Generative AI and Large Language Models on Cloud Platforms
Scalable AI/ML Model Training and Serving Infrastructure
AI Agents and Autonomous Systems for Data Workflows
AI-Native Application Development and Architecture
Multimodal Data Analysis with Foundation Models
MLOps, LLMOps, and Model Governance at Scale
Applications and Evolution of Cloud and Big Data Computing
Cloud and Big Data for IoT, Edge Computing, and Emerging Domains
Industry Practices, Business Transformation, and Case Studies
Sustainable and Green Cloud and Big Data Computing
Future Trends: Ethical AI, Quantum Computing, and Societal Impact

Link: Paper Topics
Title: ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
ï»¿
ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
Call for Papers
Call for Papers
Call for Papers
The ICCBDC 2026 topics include but are not limited to the following. Authors are encouraged to submit unpublished original research works. Topics of interest for submission include, but are not limited to:
Foundations of Cloud and Big Data Systems
Cloud-Native and Distributed System Architectures
Virtualization, Containerization, and Resource Orchestration
Big Data Processing Frameworks and Query Engines
Distributed Storage and Data Management Technologies
Performance, Scalability, and Resource Management
Intelligence and Analytics on Cloud and Big Data
Advanced Analytics, AI/ML Platforms, and Data Mining
Operational Excellence: Monitoring, Automation (AIOps/MLOps), and FinOps
Security, Privacy, and Compliance in Cloud and Big Data Environments
Data Governance, Quality, and Observability
AI-Enabled Cloud and Big Data Analytics and Services
Generative AI and Large Language Models on Cloud Platforms
Scalable AI/ML Model Training and Serving Infrastructure
AI Agents and Autonomous Systems for Data Workflows
AI-Native Application Development and Architecture
Multimodal Data Analysis with Foundation Models
MLOps, LLMOps, and Model Governance at Scale
Applications and Evolution of Cloud and Big Data Computing
Cloud and Big Data for IoT, Edge Computing, and Emerging Domains
Industry Practices, Business Transformation, and Case Studies
Sustainable and Green Cloud and Big Data Computing
Future Trends: Ethical AI, Quantum Computing, and Societal Impact

Visa
Visa
Visa Information
Not required for Australian, Canadian, New Zealand and US visitors, as well as several other nations, for stays of up to six months.
More Information
Immigration to the UK is becoming tougher, particularly for those seeking to work or study. The exit of the UK from the EU will take several years, but the process has commenced and entry requirements for EU nationals may change. Make sure you check the website of the UK Border Agency (www.gov.uk/check-uk-visa) or with your local British embassy or consulate for the most up-to-date information.

ICCBDC 2023
ICCBDC2023 is co-sponsored by Oxford Brookes University, United Kingdom and Birmingham City University, United Kingdom; hosted by Manchester Metropolitan University, United Kingdom. The event attracted 30 participants from various countries including the UK, Germany, China, Sweden, Italy, USA, and Ireland.
Manchester Metropolitan University, Manchester, United Kingdom |August 17-19, 2023

Prof. Darren Dancey, the head of the Department of Computing and Mathematics at Manchester Metropolitan University, delivered a warm welcome speech to all attendees. Additionally, Prof. Hong Zhu, the conference chair, made an exciting announcement that ICCBDC2024 will be held at Oxford Brookes University next year, extending an invitation for all to attend. The conference primarily focused on two important topics:
"Machine Learning Models and Data Computing
" and "
Intelligent Computing and Image Analysis in Modern Information Systems.
"

=== PAGE: https://www.iccbdc.org/ ===
Title: ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
ï»¿
ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
Welcome to ICCBDC 2026 @ University of Cambridge, Cambridge, U.K.
Big data is a rapidly expanding research area spanning the fields of computer science and information management, and has become a ubiquitous term in understanding and solving complex problems in different disciplinary fields such as engineering, applied mathematics, medicine, computational biology, healthcare, social networks, finance, business, government, education, transportation and telecommunications.
We sincerely invite you to participate
2026 the 10th  International Conference on Cloud and Big Data Computing (ICCBDC 2026)
. It'll be held at
University of Cambridge, Cambridge, United Kingdom during August 13-15, 2026
.
Since 2017, ICCBDC has been an annual event which was held in many places, University of Northumbria London Campus, Barcelona, St Anne's College in University of Oxford, Birmingham City University, Manchester Metropolitan University, Oxford Brookes University and University of Manchester. In 2026, ICCBDC will be held in Cambridge. The University of Cambridge is rich in history - its famous Colleges and University buildings attract visitors from all over the world.
Conference Proceedings
ICCBDC 2026 welcomes author submission of papers concerning any branch of the Cloud and Big Data, Communication Systems, and other subjects. The subjects include Big Data Algorithms, Applications and Services, Cloud and Big Data for Internet of Things (IoT),
More Topics
All academic papers will be submitted to the technical committee for double-blind peer review according to the reviewing policies. After proper registration and presentation, will be published in
ICCBDC Conference Proceedings
,
which will be archived in the
ACM Digital Library
, and be indexed by
Ei Compendex and Scopus
and submitted to be reviewed by Thomson Reuters Conference Proceedings Citation Index (ISI Web of Science).

Title: ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK

ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
Welcome
Letter from Conference Chair
Welcome Letter from Conference Chair
We sincerely invite you to participate 2026 the 10th International Conference on Cloud and Big Data Computing.  It'll be held at University of Cambridge, Cambridge, United Kingdom during August 13-15, 2026.
The goal is to establish an international forum for engineers and scientists to present their ideas and experiences in the fields of Cloud and Big Data Computing like we always do.
The ICCBDC 2026 topics include but are not limited to the following. Authors are encouraged to submit unpublished original research works in one of the following topics.
Scope and Topics
Foundations of Cloud and Big Data Systems
Cloud-Native and Distributed System Architectures
Virtualization, Containerization, and Resource Orchestration
Big Data Processing Frameworks and Query Engines
Distributed Storage and Data Management Technologies
Performance, Scalability, and Resource Management
Intelligence and Analytics on Cloud and Big Data
Advanced Analytics, AI/ML Platforms, and Data Mining
Operational Excellence: Monitoring, Automation (AIOps/MLOps), and FinOps
Security, Privacy, and Compliance in Cloud and Big Data Environments
Data Governance, Quality, and Observability
AI-Enabled Cloud and Big Data Analytics and Services
Generative AI and Large Language Models on Cloud Platforms
Scalable AI/ML Model Training and Serving Infrastructure
AI Agents and Autonomous Systems for Data Workflows
AI-Native Application Development and Architecture
Multimodal Data Analysis with Foundation Models
MLOps, LLMOps, and Model Governance at Scale
Applications and Evolution of Cloud and Big Data Computing
Cloud and Big Data for IoT, Edge Computing, and Emerging Domains
Industry Practices, Business Transformation, and Case Studies
Sustainable and Green Cloud and Big Data Computing
Future Trends: Ethical AI, Quantum Computing, and Societal Impact

If you wish to submit a paper to ICCBDC 2026, please use the following Online submission System.
Â» Click Here to Log in Online System to submit your paper (.pdf)
Paper Submission:
Authors are invited to submit an unpublished original work that must not have been submitted to an other conference, workshop or journal. All papers will be peer-reviewed by members of the Program Committee and be evaluated for originality, significance of the contribution, technical correctness and presentation.
Formatting and Publication Instructions:
Conference papers need to be prepared according to conference proceedings format.
Link to Manuscript Templates for Conference Proceedings
Review Criteria:
The degree to which the paper deals with the above issues is fundamental to a successful review and selection of the paper. The most frequent cause of rejections of submitted papers is a lack of new results. Duplicated submissions to concurrent conferences must be avoided. All submissions will be reviewed by the Program Committee on the basis of technical quality, relevance to scope of the conference, originality, significance, and clarity.
Relevance
:	Is the topic of the paper relevant to the scope of ICCBDC? Does it show the potential to stimulate interactive discussion?
Originality
:	How novel and innovative is the paper?
Significance
:	Does the paper make a valuable contribution to the theory or the practice of Cloud and Big Data Computing?
Clarity
: Is the paper well presented and organised?
Accepted papers will be published in the conference proceedings. All manuscripts are submitted as full papers and are reviewed based on their scientific merit. The reviewing process is confidential. All paper submissions will be carefully reviewed by TPC experts and reviews will be returned to the author(s) with comments to ensure the high quality of the accepted papers. The accepted papers must be revised, taking into consideration the referees' comments and suggestions, before inclusion in the conference proceedings.

Refunds Policy

Attendants who cancel their registration until May 15, 2026 will obtain a 70% refund of the paid fee.
After May 15, 2026 no cancellations are possible. Registration fees covering the publication of one or more papers CANNOT be cancelled.
Personal Reason
The ICCBDC cannot refund or pay any compensation where could not attend the conference is prevented by reason of circumstances which amount to âpersonal reasonâ. Such as travel difficulties, visa problems, health issues, financial default etc.
Force Majeure
The ICCBDC cannot accept responsibility, refund or pay any compensation where the performance of the conference is prevented or affected by reason of circumstances which amount to âforce majeureâ.
Circumstances amounting to âforce majeureâ include any event which we could not, even with all due care, foresee or avoid. Such circumstances include the fire, flood, explosion, storm or other weather damage, break-in, criminal damage, riots or civil strife, industrial action, natural or nuclear disaster, fire, adverse weather conditions, war or threat of war, actual or threatened terrorist activity, epidemic and all similar situations beyond our control.
Attention!
For safety consideration, please take good care of your belongings in the public places. Please bring your name badge for entering the conference hall. Please do not lend your name badge to people who are not involved with the conference. Please do not take irrelevant people to enter the conference rooms. The Organizers shall not be held responsibility for any loss of personal finance or belongings.

Link: Venue Location
Title: ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
ï»¿
ICCBDC2026 - Cambridge, UK
Venue
Location
Conference Venue
University of Cambridge
The University of Cambridge is rich in history - its famous Colleges and University buildings attract visitors from all over the world. But the University's museums and collections also hold many treasures which give an exciting insight into some of the scholarly activities, both past and present, of the University's academics and students.
The University of Cambridge is one of the oldest universities in the world and one of the largest in the United Kingdom. Its reputation for outstanding academic achievement is known world-wide and reflects the intellectual achievement of its students, as well as the world-class original research carried out by the staff of the University and the Colleges. Its reputation is endorsed by the Quality Assurance Agency and by other external reviewers of learning and teaching, such as External Examiners. These high standards are the result of both the learning opportunities offered at Cambridge and by its extensive resources, including libraries, museums and other collections. Teaching consists not only of lectures, seminars and practical classes led by people who are world experts in their field, but also more personalised teaching arranged through the Colleges. Many opportunities exist for students to interact with scholars of all levels, both formally and informally.
